name: OWASP starter policy
note: A STARTING POINT, not an official mapping. These rules approximate control areas from the OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026 (ASI01-ASI10), the OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications (2025), and the Agentic Security Initiative's Threats & Mitigations research (T1-T15) that fed the 2026 list - expressed over Halo Runtime Record v0.1 fields. Traceability is the cross-cutting mitigation: a tamper-evident record of what the agent did is the forensic basis the ASI list assumes. ASI08 (Cascading Agent Failures) and ASI09 (Human-Agent Trust Exploitation) are runtime/organizational risks a record policy cannot assert, so they are deliberately not covered here. Rename tools/fields to match your agent and your buyer's contract. Run: halo policy <chain.jsonl> owasp-starter.json  (or: halo report <chain.jsonl> --policy owasp-starter.json).

rules.source: OWASP ASI 2026 cross-cutting (forensic traceability) / Agentic T8 Repudiation & Untraceability
rules.description: Every recorded action carries a timestamp, so it is attributable and non-repudiable. The tamper-evident chain is the control; this rule asserts the evidence exists.

rules.source: OWASP ASI03 / Identity and Privilege Abuse (+ LLM06 Excessive Agency)
rules.description: Consequential real-world actions (writes, network egress) must carry an explicit authorization decision, never run unattributed.

rules.source: OWASP ASI03 / Identity and Privilege Abuse (+ Agentic T3 Privilege Compromise)
rules.description: Security-category write actions must be human-approved, not auto-allowed by the agent.

rules.source: OWASP ASI02 / Tool Misuse and Exploitation (+ Agentic T15 HITL bypass) (TEMPLATE)
rules.description: TEMPLATE — money movement must be human-approved. Rename action.tool to your real high-impact tools (transfer_funds, issue_refund, ...) or delete if not applicable.

rules.source: OWASP ASI02 / Tool Misuse and Exploitation (+ Agentic T2)
rules.description: Any action the recorder marked CRITICAL is treated as out of policy until a human reviews it.

rules.source: OWASP ASI01 / Agent Goal Hijack (+ LLM01 Prompt Injection)
rules.description: No prompt-injection threat may appear unmitigated in the record.

rules.source: OWASP LLM02 / Sensitive Information Disclosure (supports ASI03 credential hygiene)
rules.description: No credential or sensitive identifier may ever land in a record.
Network actions must not carry SSNs off the boundary.

rules.source: OWASP ASI06 / Memory and Context Poisoning (+ LLM04 Data Poisoning) (TEMPLATE)
rules.description: TEMPLATE — expects evidence that a source-integrity / provenance check ran before the agent acted on external content. Rename action.tool to your real integrity-check step, or delete if not applicable.

rules.source: OWASP ASI10 / Rogue Agents (purpose deviation is detectable only if purpose is declared)
rules.description: Every action that touches personal data declares why (purpose limitation).

rules.source: OWASP ASI04 / Agentic Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
rules.description: Every record carries the agent version that acted, so a supply-chain question (which build did this?) is answerable by column, not recollection.

rules.source: OWASP ASI07 / Insecure Inter-Agent Communication (+ ASI10 Rogue Agents)
rules.description: Every action is attributed to a specific agent id - the basis for reconstructing who did what when several agents share a pipeline.
TEMPLATE - agent-triggered code execution must be human-approved. Rename action.tool to your real execution tools (execute_code, run_script, ...) or delete if not applicable.
